findings: Destination Port distributions exhibit strong class separation with DDoS class showing a dominant single value and zero entropy.
High Jensen-Shannon divergence (0.7315) confirms significant distributional shift between BENIGN and DDoS classes.
No evidence of abrupt temporal shifts or instability within the local evidence scope.
Destination Port in the DDoS class exhibits near-constant behavior with zero entropy and a dominant single value ratio of 1.0.
BENIGN class shows higher cardinality and entropy, indicating more variation in Destination Port.
High Jensen-Shannon divergence (0.7315) confirms a strong distributional shift between classes.
Structural summary indicates a high variance imbalance consistent with the hypothesis.
Destination Port feature exhibits a marked distributional shift between BENIGN and DDoS classes with high class separation and low entropy in DDoS.
Shortcut verification confirms moderate shortcut-like predictive leverage of Destination Port alone for identifying DDoS class within evidence e33.
